metadata.description: Geophysical database for nuclear blast/tsunami simulation
extensions: risk_score (low altitude × low slope × high Manning)

bathymetry.notes: Seto Inland Sea — semi-enclosed shallow sea

seismic_context.notes: Sheltered by Seto Inland Sea; Nankai megathrust ~300 km south could generate <2m tsunami here
Unzen volcano nearby (1792 collapse caused 15m tsunami, 15000 deaths)
No local seismic source but exposed to Pacific Ring of Fire tsunamis
Low seismicity; primary risk is anthropogenic (nuclear test legacy)

seismic_context.tsunami_risk: moderate (Atlantic transoceanic + Cumbre Vieja flank collapse hypothesis)
seismic_context.notes: Low local seismicity; Ramapo Fault System nearby. Sandy 2012 storm surge = useful analog

bathymetry.notes: Chesapeake Bay vector, ~160 km from ocean

seismic_context.notes: 2011 M5.8 Mineral VA cracked Washington Monument. Not a tsunami zone.

seismic_context.tsunami_risk: low (storm surge dominant — Thames Barrier protects)
seismic_context.notes: 1607 Bristol Channel flood may have been a tsunami. Storm surge is primary hazard.
Negligible seismicity

seismic_context.tsunami_risk: moderate (East Sea / Sea of Japan tsunamigenic)
seismic_context.notes: Historical 1983 Sea of Japan tsunami reached Korean east coast

seismic_context.zone: Dead Sea Transform Fault (~70 km east)
seismic_context.tsunami_risk: moderate (Levantine basin; 1956 Greek tsunami reached Israel)
seismic_context.notes: Historic tsunamis: 551 AD Beirut, 1303 Crete-Levant. DSF rupture overdue.

bathymetry.notes: Asymmetric: Pacific (steep) vs Taiwan Strait (shallow shelf)

seismic_context.notes: 1999 Chi-Chi M7.6 (2400 deaths). Ryukyu Trench tsunamigenic. 2024 Hualien M7.4.
Strategic: Northern Fleet base at Severomorsk 25 km away

bathymetry.notes: Golden Gate up to 113m — exceptional depth

seismic_context.zone: San Andreas Fault System (transform plate boundary)
seismic_context.tsunami_risk: high (Cascadia subduction + local; 2011 Tohoku reached SF)
seismic_context.notes: 1906 M7.9 (~3000 deaths), 1989 Loma Prieta M6.9. Hayward Fault overdue. Marina District liquefaction zone.

seismic_context.zone: Triple junction: Pacific-Philippine Sea-Eurasian / North American
seismic_context.notes: 1923 Great Kanto M7.9 (140,000 deaths). 2011 Tohoku tsunami 40m at Miyako. Nankai Trough megathrust expected next 30 years.

seismic_context.tsunami_risk: low-moderate (East China Sea / Ryukyu via shallow shelf)
seismic_context.notes: Subsidence 1-3 cm/year worsens vulnerability. ECS shelf dissipates tsunamis.

seismic_context.tsunami_risk: moderate-high (Makran subduction north, 2004 Sumatra waves reached west India)
seismic_context.notes: Koyna induced seismicity. 1945 Makran M8.1 tsunami reached Mumbai. Sea-level rise + monsoon = compound flooding.
1945 M8.1 tsunami: 4000+ deaths, 13m waves at Pasni. Makran is severely understudied.
Carmel Fault passes through Haifa Bay; refineries are vulnerable

seismic_context.tsunami_risk: low (Black Sea semi-enclosed, but 1927 Crimea M6.8 tsunami)
seismic_context.notes: Strategic Black Sea port; ongoing war zone
1946 Aleutian tsunami: 173 deaths in Hawaii. 1960 Chile tsunami: 61 deaths in Hilo. 2011 Tohoku: damage in Hawaii.
2003 Boumerdes M6.8 caused Mediterranean tsunami
Fogo volcano flank collapse risk
365 AD Crete earthquake destroyed Alexandria
2004 Indian Ocean tsunami reached Mombasa
Direct South Indian cyclone exposure
1755 Lisbon tsunami hit Moroccan coast
2004 tsunami: 300 deaths in Somalia
1988 Spitak M6.8: 25000 deaths
Below sea level coast
1762 Arakan M8.8 caused tsunami in Bay of Bengal
1970 Bhola cyclone storm surge: 300000-500000 deaths
1976 Tangshan M7.6: 240000-650000 deaths nearby

seismic_context.zone: East Sea / Sea of Japan tsunamigenic
seismic_context.notes: Exposed to Japan-side tsunamis
1983 Sea of Japan tsunami affected Korean coast
2004 tsunami: 10000+ deaths India, 3-6m waves Chennai
Subsidence -25cm/yr. 2004 Sumatra distant. New capital Nusantara planned.
2004 Boxing Day tsunami: 30m waves Banda Aceh
Major faults beneath city; M7+ overdue
Predicted Nankai megathrust: 5m tsunami in Osaka Bay
1923 Great Kanto M7.9 hit Yokohama hard
551 AD tsunami destroyed Beirut. 2020 port explosion = blast calibration data.
Lowest country in world. 2004 tsunami: 50% of Malé submerged
2008 Cyclone Nargis storm surge 5m: 138000 deaths
2015 Gorkha M7.8: 9000 deaths
2007 Cyclone Gonu: 5m waves Muscat
Manila Trench tsunami could affect South China Sea basin
Protected by Sumatra/Borneo. No significant tsunami history.
2004 tsunami: 31000 deaths Sri Lanka, 5-11m waves
Subsidence 2cm/yr. 2004 tsunami hit Phuket (Andaman side), not Bangkok directly
1948 Ashgabat M7.3: 110000 deaths
Major M7+ earthquake expected within decades. 1509 'Lesser Day of Judgment' tsunami.
2020 Samos tsunami affected Izmir
2003 Boumerdes Algeria tsunami reached French Med coast
365 AD Crete M8.5 tsunami devastated entire Mediterranean. ~1600 BC Santorini caldera collapse: 35-100m tsunami
Volcanic activity (Reykjanes 2021-present)
79 AD Vesuvius eruption (Pompeii). Campi Flegrei uplifting now. 1908 Messina M7.1 tsunami nearby: 80000 deaths
MOSE flood barrier active since 2020. Acqua alta routine.

1755 Lisbon: 6-15m tsunami, 60000+ deaths. KEY HISTORICAL CALIBRATION EVENT.
1977 Vrancea M7.4: 1578 deaths Bucharest
1824 flood: 4m above sea level. Flood barrier completed 2011.
1983 Sea of Japan tsunami affected Russian coast
1927 Yalta M6.8 tsunami in Black Sea
Cascadia M9.0 overdue (last 1700 AD). Tsunami modeling: 5-8m West Vancouver Island
1929 Grand Banks tsunami: 28 deaths Newfoundland
2010 Maule M8.8 tsunami: 525 deaths. 1730 Valparaiso quake & tsunami devastating.
1906 Ecuador-Colombia M8.8 tsunami: 1500 deaths
1976 Guatemala M7.5: 23000 deaths
2010 Haiti M7.0: 220000 deaths
1692 Port Royal earthquake: 2/3 of city sunk into sea
1985 Mexico City M8.0: 10000+ deaths due to soft lake-bed amplification
1972 Managua M6.2: 10000 deaths
1746 Lima-Callao M8.6 tsunami: 6000 deaths Callao. Major M9 event overdue.
1918 Mona Passage M7.1 tsunami: 116 deaths
1994 Northridge M6.7. San Andreas 'Big One' overdue.
Harvey 2017 storm surge analog
Hurricane Andrew 1992 analog. Sea level rise critical.
